# UC-13.7.6 · Fluent Bit Output Plugin Flush Latency P95 Tail-Pain Detection, Per-Destination RTT Decomposition, and Backpressure-Onset Forecasting

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Performance, Capacity &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We time how long each stop on the log delivery route holds onto a package, especially the worst slow cases, and we notice when those slow cases start speeding up in a way that means a pile-up is coming before the mailbox overflows.*

---

## Description

Detects Fluent Bit output plugin flush latency tail pain at approximately ninety-fifth and ninety-ninth percentiles per destination—Splunk HTTP Event Collector, Kafka, Amazon S3, Elasticsearch, OpenTelemetry Protocol exporters, and Amazon CloudWatch Logs—by fusing scraped fluentbit:metrics timers, structured fluentbit:log signals, optional fluentbit:http_timing decompositions into network round trip time versus serialization versus queue wait, and Splunk splunkd_ui_access collector timing, then computing peer cluster medians, tail-to-median gap ratios, and flush latency rate-of-change from streamstats to forecast backpressure onset before mem_buf_limit or storage.max_chunks_up exhausts on edge agents.

## Value

Platform and telemetry leaders gain minutes to hours of lead time when forwarders still admit logs yet flush durations stretch in the tail, preventing silent chunk backlog on small hosts and guarding revenue-impacting analytics that depend on timely operational logs while finance reviewers see defensible Splunk-centric evidence tied to per-output service targets.

## Implementation

Enable Fluent Bit monitoring scrapes into sourcetype=fluentbit:metrics, ship fluentbit:log and optional fluentbit:http_timing, allow index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd_ui_access for HEC paths under governance, maintain fluentbit_output_flush_slo_targets.csv and fluentd_node_inventory.csv, schedule observability_uc_13_7_6_fluent_bit_flush_latency_tail_roc every ten to fifteen minutes with earliest=-4h@h, route page and warn to platform logging on-call, and archive weekly evidence exports with chunk_id and worker_id drilldowns.

## Evidence

Saved search observability_uc_13_7_6_fluent_bit_flush_latency_tail_roc with ten to fifteen minute schedule; observability dashboard drilldowns on fluentbit sourcetypes; routing to platform logging on-call; weekly CSV snapshots of worst tail_gap_ratio rows archived to a restricted index.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

In a lab namespace, add controlled latency to a non-production Splunk HEC endpoint or Elasticsearch bulk path, confirm fluentbit:metrics or fluentbit:http_timing arms raise flush_seconds_p95 with elevated flush_roc_per_min while sibling error-rate alarms remain quiet, and verify observability_uc_13_7_6_fluent_bit_flush_latency_tail_roc returns warn or page for the expected output within twenty minutes.

### Negative scenario

After removing artificial delay when medians and ninetieth percentiles return to documented baselines, splunkd_ui_access timing normalizes, ROC flattens, and filtering the saved search to page and warn yields zero rows for thirty consecutive schedules.

## Detailed Implementation

### Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with the observability pipeline engineering lead, the telemetry platform team that scrapes Prometheus and OpenTelemetry from Kubernetes DaemonSets and IoT gateways, network operations partners who own datacenter and cloud egress paths, and FinOps-aligned capacity reviewers who approve increases to Splunk HTTP Event Collector throughput, Kafka broker clusters, Amazon Simple Storage Service object-ingest profiles, Elasticsearch rollover economics, OpenTelemetry Collector fan-in, and Amazon CloudWatch Logs ingestion budgets. This use case isolates Fluent Bit output plugin flush latency with emphasis on ninety-fifth and ninety-ninth percentile tail pain rather than median comfort, because edge agents on small virtual machines, constrained containers, and brownfield IoT gateways remain healthy at the fifty-fifth percentile while a widening tail silently consumes scheduler quanta, elongates chunk lifetimes, and foreshadows chunk backlog long before mem_buf_limit or storage.max_chunks_up alarms from UC-13.7.5 cross red lines. Tail latency answers a distinct reliability question from UC-13.7.1 buffer overflow and retry exhaustion, which foregrounds saturated queues and give-up loops after outcomes have hardened, not the minutes-earlier story where each flush simply takes longer yet still commits. It is deliberately not UC-13.7.2 output plugin error rate analytics, because a destination can keep HTTP 200 success while acknowledgements or broker commits stretch through overloaded thread pools. It is not UC-13.7.3 input versus output throughput mismatch, which emphasizes bytes-per-second ledger imbalance rather than end-to-end flush time probability mass in the right tail. It is not UC-13.7.4 worker or supervisor crash detection, where processes restart and tails may look artificially short during outages. It is not UC-13.7.5 memory buffer utilization and minutes-to-full capacity forecasting anchored on byte gauges, although flush latency growth often foreshadows that curve and should be read together without merging narratives. It is not UC-13.7.9 TLS error-focused correlation, because certificate and cipher failures produce characteristic error lanes rather than smooth latency creep.

Fluent Bit ships a compact C engine with per-input, filter, and output task model. Output plugins such as splunk, kafka, es, s3, opentelemetry (OTLP exporters), and cloudwatch_logs serialize chunks, negotiate connection pools, and await remote acceptance. The Prometheus text or JSON surface exposed under administration monitoring configuration summarizes counters including fluentbit_output_proc_records_total, fluentbit_output_proc_bytes_total, fluentbit_output_errors_total, fluentbit_output_retries_total, fluentbit_filter_drop_records_total, and fluentbit_uptime, plus histogram or summary derivatives when your build enables them. Practitioners must not conflate counter deltas with latency: you need either exported flush-duration histograms, derived spans from structured logs, or externally measured HTTP timing logs to estimate how long each destination holds a chunk in flight. Network round trip time, serialization and compression, and internal queue wait before the socket write are intentionally separated in this UC so responders open the correct runbook: a network regression toward Splunk load balancers differs from expensive gzip toward Elasticsearch or oversized batches toward Kafka.

Per-destination contracts differ materially. Splunk HTTP Event Collector acknowledges event receipt and may surface ack identifiers in advanced configurations; Splunk operators often approximate server think time using splunkd_ui_access lines tied to collector paths even when agents do not emit identical timers, with care to avoid double counting overlapping proxies. Apache Kafka output plugins await produce acknowledgements and can stall when partitions hot-spot or brokers honor long replications. Elasticsearch outputs block on bulk thread pools, disk watermarks, and refresh intervals. S3 outputs encounter DNS, signature, multipart stability, and regional endpoint latency variance. OTLP exporters face gRPC windowing, HTTP/2 flow control, and downstream OpenTelemetry Collector admission. CloudWatch outputs batch PutLogEvents and contend with Amazon Web Services throttling semantics distinct from plain HTTPS latency.

RBAC and privacy matter because high-resolution timing logs can echo payload metadata, endpoint hostnames, and tenant identifiers. Scrapers need narrowly scoped service accounts for pod metrics endpoints. Splunk _internal access for splunkd_ui_access remains platform-operator data with tight role constraints. Redact raw customer payloads from pager templates while preserving numeric timers and chunked identities such as chunk_id and worker_id for cross-referencing vendor traces.

Index design: land sourcetype=fluentbit:metrics for JSON or Prometheus-normalized series scraped from fluentbit:metrics HTTP listeners, sourcetype=fluentbit:log for structured or unstructured engine diagnostics that mention flush timing, sourcetype=fluentbit:http_timing for optional OpenTelemetry-style or bespoke timing events emitted by wrappers around curl-compatible outputs, and index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd_ui_access for Splunk-side acknowledgement timing context. Maintain fluentbit_output_flush_slo_targets.csv keyed on output with flush_p95_warn_s, flush_p95_page_s, and dest_family columns plus optional notes for Kafka, Splunk, S3, Elasticsearch, OTLP, and CloudWatch shapes. Reuse fluentd_node_inventory.csv from UC-13.7.1 style fleets so host rows carry cluster_tier, owner_team, and environment without ad hoc typing during incidents.

Risk briefing: short spikes in tail latency during approved maintenance windows may be benign when change_ticket_id metadata is present and retries remain bounded; use join enrichment and calendar correlation before paging application teams. Canary clusters running aggressive debug logging can dominate percentiles without production customer impact if environment labels route severities to platform chat. Mis-clocked agents distort rate-of-change math; monitor stratum drift between Fluent Bit hosts and Splunk indexers. Very low traffic outputs produce unstable percentiles in narrow windows; require minimum event counts or widen spans in the saved search schedule.

Differentiation recap: p95 and p99 flush tail pain, decomposition proxies for network versus serialization versus queue wait, flush_seconds_p95 rate of change via streamstats windows for backpressure onset forecasting, and explicit per-output blame across Splunk HEC, Kafka, S3, Elasticsearch, OTLP, and CloudWatch lanes, not buffer byte percent dashboards alone, not generic error rate burn, not throughput imbalance ledgers, not crash loops, not TLS-only narratives. Fluent Bit upstream backpressure mechanics are also summarized in community documentation such as https://github.com/fluent/fluent-bit/wiki/Backpressure for readers who want vendor-neutral context beyond Splunk onboarding guides.

### Step 2 — Configure data collection

Stand up four complementary multisearch arms plus enrichment lookups so a silent single sourcetype never hides genuine tail regression during black-swan network events or partial scrape outages.

Fluent Bit metrics arm: enable HTTP Server or compatible Prometheus scraping as described in Fluent Bit monitoring documentation. Schedule Splunk Technical Add-on style scripted inputs or OpenTelemetry Collector receivers to POST normalized JSON into sourcetype=fluentbit:metrics, preserving labels that identify host, kubernetes.pod.name, output plugin alias, pipeline sections, and scraped __name__ values. Map histogram buckets into flush_seconds_p95 when your Prometheus recorder exposes them; otherwise land task_duration_seconds-style gauges per output. Include fluentbit_output_proc_records_total and fluentbit_output_retries_total deltas in dashboards even though this UC prioritizes timers, because sudden divergences between records flushed and retry counters still contextualize tails.

Fluent Bit log arm: ship container stdout and stderr with sourcetype=fluentbit:log, preserving multiline scheduler messages. Add optional STRUCTURED_METADATA sidecars that print chunk_id, output name, tag, worker_id, and measured flush milliseconds when you patch custom logging hooks; this dramatically tightens forensic joins to metrics arms without guessing regexes on unstructured noise.

HTTP timing arm: implement sourcetype=fluentbit:http_timing through a thin wrapper or service mesh access log that records total_request_ms, tcp_connect_ms, body_encode_ms, and sched_wait_ms for each destination family. For Kafka, include broker leader hints. For S3, include bucket and region. For Elasticsearch, include cluster name. For OTLP, include exporter protocol grpc versus http/protobuf. This arm is optional at crawl maturity but strongly recommended for walk-tier deployments because decomposition into network versus serialization versus queue wait depends on trustworthy client-side spans or timing proxies.

Splunk HEC acknowledgement arm: where governance permits, forward splunkd_ui_access with uri paths referencing services collector endpoints. Parse ackId when present to align with Fluent Bit splunk output chatter, understanding Splunk may record gateway time rather than end-to-end indexer commit. Pair with MC or datastore queues elsewhere when investigating tail growth isolated to Splunk.

Lookup maintenance: curate fluentbit_output_flush_slo_targets.csv with conservative warn and page seconds informed by destination baselines, per-tenant contracts, and observed p50 medians from historical weeks. Review quarterly after major Fluent Bit upgrades or cloud region migrations.

props.conf guidance: honor ISO-8601 _time from scrapers, set TRUNCATE high for wide Prometheus lines, and strip ANSI color from container logs. transforms.conf can alias kubernetes.pod.name into host when host is empty on DaemonSets.

Pre-save validation: induce a deliberate non-production slowdown on a single output, confirm flush_seconds_p95 rises in Splunk within two scrape intervals, confirm splunkd_ui_access arm moves for Splunk-only tests, and confirm fluentbit:http_timing arms show inflated total_request_ms isolated to the troubled plugin family.

Example operator helpers for design reviews:

```bash
curl -sS http://127.0.0.1:2020/api/v1/metrics | head -n 40
kubectl -n logging logs daemonset/fluent-bit --tail=200 | rg -i "flush|task|retry"
kubectl -n logging get pods -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluent-bit -o wide
```

Dashboards: per-output heatmaps of flush_seconds_p95, sparklines for flush_roc_per_min, stacked area for rtt_seconds versus serialize_seconds versus queue_wait_seconds, and single-value worst tail_gap_ratio tiles by cluster and output.

### Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as observability_uc_13_7_6_fluent_bit_flush_latency_tail_roc with a ten to fifteen minute schedule during steady-state production, dispatch earliest=-4h@h, dispatch latest=now, and alert when severity is page or warn. Throttle duplicate cluster, host, output rows for twenty-five minutes unless severity escalates from warn to page or flush_roc_per_min doubles. Include recommended_action, lane, dest_family, and tail_gap_ratio in pager text so incident commanders open destination runbooks without rerunning ad hoc searches.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans fluentbit:metrics scrape lines, fluentbit:log structured hints, fluentbit:http_timing spans, and splunkd_ui_access HEC timing so one missing lane does not hide cross-destination regression. coalesce ladders absorb field renames across Prometheus label sets and JSON exporters. eventstats derives cluster-level peer_flush_p50 and peer_flush_p95 distributions per output to expose tail gap versus typical case. streamstats windows emit flush_roc_per_min rate-of-change estimates that act as front-foot backpressure onset indicators when medians still look calm. join attaches SLO targets from fluentbit_output_flush_slo_targets.csv and business ownership from fluentd_node_inventory.csv. A second join runs tstats against the Splunk CIM Performance model for cpu_load_percent contextualization on the same host keys, surfacing when CPU saturation might explain serialization growth versus network issues. case maps combined absolute thresholds, SLO breaches, tail_gap_ratio multiples, and ROC velocity into page versus warn versus info tiers.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.7.6 Fluent Bit output flush latency p95 tail-pain ROC backpressure onset per Splunk HEC Kafka S3 ES OTLP CloudWatch. Tunables earliest=-4h@h roc_page=0.35 roc_warn=0.12")`
| multisearch
    [ search (index=fluent OR index=observability OR index=platform OR index=kubernetes) sourcetype=fluentbit:metrics earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,k8s_pod_name,pod_name,kubernetes.pod.name,name,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval pipeline=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(pipeline,pipeline_name,service_name,"fb_default"))))
      | eval output=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(output,plugin_name,plugin,o_name,section,"unknown_output"))))
      | eval endpoint=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(endpoint,host_port,splunk_host,hec_host,"unknown_ep"))))
      | eval tag=trim(toString(coalesce(tag,match_tag,"*")))
      | eval chunk_id=trim(toString(coalesce(chunk_id,chunk,task_id,"metric_chunk")))
      | eval worker_id=trim(toString(coalesce(worker_id,worker,thread_id,"0")))
      | eval name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(__name__,metric_name,""))))
      | where match(name,"fluentbit_output_proc_records_total|fluentbit_output_errors_total|fluentbit_output_retries_total|fluentbit_output_proc_bytes_total|fluentbit_filter_drop_records_total|fluentbit_uptime") OR like(name,"fluentbit_%_duration%") OR like(name,"fluentbit_%_latency%")
      | eval task_dur=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(task_duration_seconds,output_duration_seconds,flush_time_seconds,http_request_duration_seconds,"0")),10)
      | eval rtt_seconds=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(http_net_roundtrip_seconds,network_rtt_seconds,tcp_rtt_seconds,"0")),10)
      | eval serialize_seconds=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(json_pack_seconds,codec_seconds,pack_seconds,"0")),10)
      | eval queue_wait_seconds=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(queue_wait_seconds,sched_wait_seconds,storage_queue_wait_seconds,"0")),10)
      | eval flush_seconds_p95=round(coalesce(if(task_dur>0.0005,task_dur,null()),if(rtt_seconds+serialize_seconds+queue_wait_seconds>0,rtt_seconds+serialize_seconds+queue_wait_seconds,null()),task_dur),6)
      | eval lane="fb_metrics_scrape"
      | fields _time host pipeline output endpoint tag name chunk_id worker_id flush_seconds_p95 rtt_seconds serialize_seconds queue_wait_seconds lane ]
    [ search (index=fluent OR index=kubernetes OR index=observability) sourcetype=fluentbit:log earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where match(lr,"flush|chunk|output|task|retry|scheduler|ingest") AND !match(lr,"tls.error|certificate|x509|handshake")
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,pod_name,k8s_pod_name,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval pipeline=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(pipeline,"fb_default"))))
      | eval output=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(output,plugin_name,section,"unknown_output"))))
      | eval endpoint=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(endpoint,"unknown_ep"))))
      | eval tag=trim(toString(coalesce(tag,"*")))
      | rex field=lr max_match=1 "(?i)flush[^0-9]{0,28}(?<fms>\d+(?:\.\d+)?)\s*ms"
      | eval flush_ms=coalesce(tonumber(fms,10),tonumber(tostring(coalesce(flush_latency_ms,flush_time_ms,"0")),10))
      | eval flush_seconds_p95=round(if(flush_ms>0,flush_ms/1000,tonumber(tostring(coalesce(flush_seconds,"0")),10)),6)
      | eval rtt_seconds=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(net_rtt_ms,"0")),10)/1000
      | eval serialize_seconds=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(serialize_ms,"0")),10)/1000
      | eval queue_wait_seconds=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(queue_wait_ms,"0")),10)/1000
      | eval chunk_id=trim(toString(coalesce(chunk_id,chunk,"log_chunk")))
      | eval worker_id=trim(toString(coalesce(worker_id,"0")))
      | eval name="fluentbit_engine_log"
      | eval lane="fb_stderr_struct"
      | fields _time host pipeline output endpoint tag name chunk_id worker_id flush_seconds_p95 rtt_seconds serialize_seconds queue_wait_seconds lane ]
    [ search (index=fluent OR index=platform OR index=main) sourcetype=fluentbit:http_timing earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,src_host,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval pipeline=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(pipeline,"fb_http_timing"))))
      | eval output=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(output,dest_plugin,kafka_cluster_id,s3_bucket,es_cluster,"unknown_output"))))
      | eval endpoint=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(endpoint,url_host,http_host,arn_host,"unknown_ep"))))
      | eval tag=trim(toString(coalesce(tag,"*")))
      | eval flush_seconds_p95=round(tonumber(tostring(coalesce(total_request_ms,http_total_ms,request_latency_ms,"0")),10)/1000,6)
      | eval rtt_seconds=round(tonumber(tostring(coalesce(tcp_connect_ms,dns_lookup_ms,"0")),10)/1000,6)
      | eval serialize_seconds=round(tonumber(tostring(coalesce(body_encode_ms,json_pack_ms,"0")),10)/1000,6)
      | eval queue_wait_seconds=round(tonumber(tostring(coalesce(sched_wait_ms,queue_before_http_ms,"0")),10)/1000,6)
      | eval chunk_id=trim(toString(coalesce(chunk_id,http_req_id,"http_chunk")))
      | eval worker_id=trim(toString(coalesce(worker_id,thread_slot,"0")))
      | eval name="output_http_span"
      | eval lane="fb_plugin_http_span"
      | fields _time host pipeline output endpoint tag name chunk_id worker_id flush_seconds_p95 rtt_seconds serialize_seconds queue_wait_seconds lane ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd_ui_access earliest=-4h@h latest=now (uri_path="*/services/collector*" OR uri="*/services/collector/event*" OR match(_raw,"/services/collector"))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,clientip,h_orig,"hec_peer"))))
      | eval pipeline="splunk_ingest_ui"
      | eval output="splunk_hec"
      | eval endpoint=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(uri,uri_path,splunkd_uri,"hec_path"))))
      | eval tag="hec"
      | rex field=_raw max_match=1 "(?i)ack[Ii]d[\"=: ]+(?<ack_id>[a-fA-F0-9-]{8,64})"
      | eval name=coalesce(ack_id,"hec_post_latency")
      | eval chunk_id=trim(toString(coalesce(ack_id,rid,"hec_rid")))
      | eval worker_id="hec_handler"
      | eval hec_ms=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(request_time_ms,duration_ms,resp_time_ms,ttms,"0")),10)
      | eval flush_seconds_p95=round(hec_ms/1000,6)
      | eval rtt_seconds=round(flush_seconds_p95*0.52,6)
      | eval serialize_seconds=round(flush_seconds_p95*0.28,6)
      | eval queue_wait_seconds=round(flush_seconds_p95*0.20,6)
      | eval lane="hec_splunkd_ui_access"
      | fields _time host pipeline output endpoint tag name chunk_id worker_id flush_seconds_p95 rtt_seconds serialize_seconds queue_wait_seconds lane ]
| eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,kubernetes_cluster,k8s_cluster_name,"undetermined"))))
| eval merge_key=strcat(cluster,"|",host,"|",output,"|",lane)
| eventstats perc95(flush_seconds_p95) AS cluster_out_p95 median(flush_seconds_p95) AS cluster_out_p50 BY cluster output
| sort 0 merge_key + _time
| streamstats window=7 current=t global=f last(flush_seconds_p95) AS prev_flush last(_time) AS prev_tick BY merge_key
| eval flush_roc_per_min=if(isnotnull(prev_tick) AND prev_tick<_time AND isnotnull(prev_flush), round((flush_seconds_p95-prev_flush)/((_time-prev_tick)/60),6), 0)
| stats max(_time) AS last_seen max(flush_seconds_p95) AS flush_seconds_p95 max(flush_roc_per_min) AS flush_roc_per_min max(rtt_seconds) AS rtt_seconds max(serialize_seconds) AS serialize_seconds max(queue_wait_seconds) AS queue_wait_seconds first(tag) AS tag first(name) AS name first(chunk_id) AS chunk_id first(worker_id) AS worker_id first(pipeline) AS pipeline first(endpoint) AS endpoint first(lane) AS lane max(cluster_out_p95) AS peer_flush_p95 max(cluster_out_p50) AS peer_flush_p50 BY cluster host output
| join type=left max=0 output
    [| inputlookup fluentbit_output_flush_slo_targets.csv
     | eval output=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(output,plugin_key,dest_key,""))))
     | fields output flush_p95_warn_s flush_p95_page_s dest_family ]
| join type=left max=0 host
    [| inputlookup fluentd_node_inventory.csv
     | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(node,host,hostname,""))))
     | fields host cluster_tier owner_team environment ]
| join type=left max=0 host
    [| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu_load FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
     | rename Performance.host AS host ]
| eval flush_p95_warn_s=coalesce(flush_p95_warn_s,2.0)
| eval flush_p95_page_s=coalesce(flush_p95_page_s,7.5)
| eval tail_gap_ratio=if(peer_flush_p50>0, round(flush_seconds_p95/peer_flush_p50,3), null())
| eval severity=case(flush_seconds_p95>=flush_p95_page_s OR flush_roc_per_min>=0.35 OR (tail_gap_ratio>=3.5 AND flush_seconds_p95>=4.0),"page", flush_seconds_p95>=flush_p95_warn_s OR flush_roc_per_min>=0.12 OR (tail_gap_ratio>=2.2 AND flush_seconds_p95>=1.8),"warn", true(),"info")
| eval recommended_action=case(severity=="page" AND match(lane,"hec_"),"inspect_splunk_hec_lb_ack_indexer_queues_increase_hec_throughput_validate_token_pool", severity=="page" AND match(output,"kafka"),"tune_kafka_linger_batch_bytes_broker_latency_partition_skew_and_compression", severity=="page" AND (match(output,"s3") OR match(dest_family,"s3")),"validate_s3_region_endpoint_sts_timeouts_multipart_limits_and_disk_spill", severity=="page" AND (match(output,"elastic") OR match(output,"es")),"check_es_bulk_threadpool_rejections_disk_watermarks_and_index_routing", severity=="page" AND (match(output,"otlp") OR match(output,"otel")),"trace_otlp_grpc_backpressure_codec_batch_and_downstream_collector_saturation", severity=="page" AND match(output,"cloudwatch"),"validate_cloudwatch_putlog_events_batching_throttling_and_iam_role_session", severity=="page","deepen_fluent_bit_output_profiler_net_vs_serialization_vs_queue_wait_raise_workers_under_cpu_budget", severity=="warn","compare_p50_to_p95_tail_expand_http_timing_logs_tune_chunk_size_vs_flush_interval", true(),"refresh_baselines_slo_lookup_and_per_output_latency_dashboards")
| table last_seen cluster host pipeline output endpoint flush_seconds_p95 peer_flush_p50 peer_flush_p95 rtt_seconds serialize_seconds queue_wait_seconds flush_roc_per_min tail_gap_ratio severity recommended_action chunk_id worker_id tag lane cluster_tier owner_team dest_family cpu_load
```


savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[observability_uc_13_7_6_fluent_bit_flush_latency_tail_roc_alert]
cron_schedule = */10 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -4h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch observability_uc_13_7_6_fluent_bit_flush_latency_tail_roc | where severity IN ("page","warn")
```

Performance: if multisearch queue time in Job Inspector exceeds platform budgets, materialize per-output percentile summaries hourly into a metrics-or summary index keyed on cluster, host, and output, then widen alert earliest windows while retaining this detailed search for deep dives. For very high cardinality Prometheus labels, drop low-value label dimensions at ingest after consulting observability governance.

### Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth always starts on the node. Compare curl http://127.0.0.1:2020/api/v1/metrics snapshots to sourcetype=fluentbit:metrics rows for the same host, output alias, and __name__ within one scrape interval. When histogram quantiles disagree, verify label rewriting, stale ServiceMonitor endpoints, and duplicated scrapers before blaming Splunk parsers.

Deep dive with kubectl -n logging logs for fluent-bit and compare flush millisecond hints in stderr to fluentbit:log severity math. Pair with tcpdump or cloud flow logs only under approved procedures when debating network round trip time versus remote service stalls.

Splunk correlation: filter splunkd_ui_access to collector paths and compare median versus tail to Splunk Monitoring Console queue indicators; if Splunk tails lengthen while Kafka tails remain calm, avoid global buffer tuning that punishes unrelated outputs.

Synthetic validation: in lab, inject artificial per-batch sleep in a non-production Elasticsearch bulk endpoint or throttle Kafka broker throughput, confirm observability_uc_13_7_6_fluent_bit_flush_latency_tail_roc elevates severity for the expected output only, then restore service and confirm return to info within two windows.

Negative test: on healthy fleet weeks after upgrade stabilization, confirm page and warn filters yield zero rows for thirty consecutive evaluations when flags match documented noise floors.

Runbook linkage: document intentional tail spikes during large binary log schema changes that temporarily inflate serialization seconds without implying impending chunk loss.

### Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — Splunk HEC tail growth with stable p50 but exploding p95: validate load balancer persistence, indexer peer acknowledgment settings, search-head versus indexer maintenance windows, and token rate policies before increasing Fluent Bit workers alone.

Case 2 — Kafka output p99 dominated by queue_wait_seconds: inspect linger milliseconds, batch bytes, compression codec CPU cost, and partition key skew; rebalance leadership only after broker health returns green.

Case 3 — Elasticsearch bulk rejections visible only in timing tails: reduce bulk payload size, widen refresh intervals under indexing team guidance, and verify disk watermarks on hot nodes.

Case 4 — S3 multipart stalls elevating flush_seconds_p95: check regional endpoint reachability, IAM role session duration, and noisy neighbor bandwidth on shared NAT gateways.

Case 5 — OTLP gRPC window pressure: validate upstream Collector memory limits, exporter batching, and HTTP/2 max frame interplay without conflating with application trace storms.

Case 6 — CloudWatch PutLogEvents throttling fingerprints: align batch sizes and retention policies with Amazon quotas while correlating tail pain to regional service health dashboards.

Case 7 — Serialize_seconds spike after Fluent Bit minor upgrade: compare compression defaults, new JSON marshalling paths, and optional WASM filter cost; roll forward with vendor release notes or pin builds under change control.

Case 8 — Network RTT regression across all outputs on one subnet: engage network operations for path mtu, east-west firewall inspection, or DNS latency shifts rather than tuning three unrelated buffer knobs.

Case 9 — Single worker_id carrying disproportionate tail latency: investigate CPU pinning, noisy cgroup throttling, or container CPU limits that serialize tasks despite multi-worker configuration.

Case 10 — Mis-tagged chunk_id correlation failures between metrics and logs: repair FIELDALIAS maps and HEC token routing so chunk_id aligns across arms for join hygiene.

Case 11 — SLO lookup stale after new output plugin rollout: refresh fluentbit_output_flush_slo_targets.csv with measured steady-state percentiles before trusting warn and page thresholds.

Case 12 — False calm when metrics scrape fails but logs still present: alert on absent heartbeat counts per host for sourcetype=fluentbit:metrics while consolidating severity only after confirming collection gaps.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists fluentbit:metrics, fluentbit:log, fluentbit:http_timing, and splunkd_ui_access arms; coalesce appears in multisearch arms; streamstats and eventstats appear; case drives severity; inputlookup SLO join and inventory join appear; tstats enriches cpu_load; final table lists twenty-three analyst columns including tail_gap_ratio, flush_roc_per_min, and per-lane context.



## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-13.7.6 Fluent Bit output flush latency p95 tail-pain ROC backpressure onset per Splunk HEC Kafka S3 ES OTLP CloudWatch. Tunables earliest=-4h@h roc_page=0.35 roc_warn=0.12")`
| multisearch
    [ search (index=fluent OR index=observability OR index=platform OR index=kubernetes) sourcetype=fluentbit:metrics earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,k8s_pod_name,pod_name,kubernetes.pod.name,name,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval pipeline=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(pipeline,pipeline_name,service_name,"fb_default"))))
      | eval output=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(output,plugin_name,plugin,o_name,section,"unknown_output"))))
      | eval endpoint=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(endpoint,host_port,splunk_host,hec_host,"unknown_ep"))))
      | eval tag=trim(toString(coalesce(tag,match_tag,"*")))
      | eval chunk_id=trim(toString(coalesce(chunk_id,chunk,task_id,"metric_chunk")))
      | eval worker_id=trim(toString(coalesce(worker_id,worker,thread_id,"0")))
      | eval name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(__name__,metric_name,""))))
      | where match(name,"fluentbit_output_proc_records_total|fluentbit_output_errors_total|fluentbit_output_retries_total|fluentbit_output_proc_bytes_total|fluentbit_filter_drop_records_total|fluentbit_uptime") OR like(name,"fluentbit_%_duration%") OR like(name,"fluentbit_%_latency%")
      | eval task_dur=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(task_duration_seconds,output_duration_seconds,flush_time_seconds,http_request_duration_seconds,"0")),10)
      | eval rtt_seconds=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(http_net_roundtrip_seconds,network_rtt_seconds,tcp_rtt_seconds,"0")),10)
      | eval serialize_seconds=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(json_pack_seconds,codec_seconds,pack_seconds,"0")),10)
      | eval queue_wait_seconds=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(queue_wait_seconds,sched_wait_seconds,storage_queue_wait_seconds,"0")),10)
      | eval flush_seconds_p95=round(coalesce(if(task_dur>0.0005,task_dur,null()),if(rtt_seconds+serialize_seconds+queue_wait_seconds>0,rtt_seconds+serialize_seconds+queue_wait_seconds,null()),task_dur),6)
      | eval lane="fb_metrics_scrape"
      | fields _time host pipeline output endpoint tag name chunk_id worker_id flush_seconds_p95 rtt_seconds serialize_seconds queue_wait_seconds lane ]
    [ search (index=fluent OR index=kubernetes OR index=observability) sourcetype=fluentbit:log earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where match(lr,"flush|chunk|output|task|retry|scheduler|ingest") AND !match(lr,"tls.error|certificate|x509|handshake")
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,pod_name,k8s_pod_name,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval pipeline=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(pipeline,"fb_default"))))
      | eval output=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(output,plugin_name,section,"unknown_output"))))
      | eval endpoint=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(endpoint,"unknown_ep"))))
      | eval tag=trim(toString(coalesce(tag,"*")))
      | rex field=lr max_match=1 "(?i)flush[^0-9]{0,28}(?<fms>\d+(?:\.\d+)?)\s*ms"
      | eval flush_ms=coalesce(tonumber(fms,10),tonumber(tostring(coalesce(flush_latency_ms,flush_time_ms,"0")),10))
      | eval flush_seconds_p95=round(if(flush_ms>0,flush_ms/1000,tonumber(tostring(coalesce(flush_seconds,"0")),10)),6)
      | eval rtt_seconds=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(net_rtt_ms,"0")),10)/1000
      | eval serialize_seconds=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(serialize_ms,"0")),10)/1000
      | eval queue_wait_seconds=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(queue_wait_ms,"0")),10)/1000
      | eval chunk_id=trim(toString(coalesce(chunk_id,chunk,"log_chunk")))
      | eval worker_id=trim(toString(coalesce(worker_id,"0")))
      | eval name="fluentbit_engine_log"
      | eval lane="fb_stderr_struct"
      | fields _time host pipeline output endpoint tag name chunk_id worker_id flush_seconds_p95 rtt_seconds serialize_seconds queue_wait_seconds lane ]
    [ search (index=fluent OR index=platform OR index=main) sourcetype=fluentbit:http_timing earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,src_host,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval pipeline=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(pipeline,"fb_http_timing"))))
      | eval output=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(output,dest_plugin,kafka_cluster_id,s3_bucket,es_cluster,"unknown_output"))))
      | eval endpoint=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(endpoint,url_host,http_host,arn_host,"unknown_ep"))))
      | eval tag=trim(toString(coalesce(tag,"*")))
      | eval flush_seconds_p95=round(tonumber(tostring(coalesce(total_request_ms,http_total_ms,request_latency_ms,"0")),10)/1000,6)
      | eval rtt_seconds=round(tonumber(tostring(coalesce(tcp_connect_ms,dns_lookup_ms,"0")),10)/1000,6)
      | eval serialize_seconds=round(tonumber(tostring(coalesce(body_encode_ms,json_pack_ms,"0")),10)/1000,6)
      | eval queue_wait_seconds=round(tonumber(tostring(coalesce(sched_wait_ms,queue_before_http_ms,"0")),10)/1000,6)
      | eval chunk_id=trim(toString(coalesce(chunk_id,http_req_id,"http_chunk")))
      | eval worker_id=trim(toString(coalesce(worker_id,thread_slot,"0")))
      | eval name="output_http_span"
      | eval lane="fb_plugin_http_span"
      | fields _time host pipeline output endpoint tag name chunk_id worker_id flush_seconds_p95 rtt_seconds serialize_seconds queue_wait_seconds lane ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd_ui_access earliest=-4h@h latest=now (uri_path="*/services/collector*" OR uri="*/services/collector/event*" OR match(_raw,"/services/collector"))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,clientip,h_orig,"hec_peer"))))
      | eval pipeline="splunk_ingest_ui"
      | eval output="splunk_hec"
      | eval endpoint=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(uri,uri_path,splunkd_uri,"hec_path"))))
      | eval tag="hec"
      | rex field=_raw max_match=1 "(?i)ack[Ii]d[\"=: ]+(?<ack_id>[a-fA-F0-9-]{8,64})"
      | eval name=coalesce(ack_id,"hec_post_latency")
      | eval chunk_id=trim(toString(coalesce(ack_id,rid,"hec_rid")))
      | eval worker_id="hec_handler"
      | eval hec_ms=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(request_time_ms,duration_ms,resp_time_ms,ttms,"0")),10)
      | eval flush_seconds_p95=round(hec_ms/1000,6)
      | eval rtt_seconds=round(flush_seconds_p95*0.52,6)
      | eval serialize_seconds=round(flush_seconds_p95*0.28,6)
      | eval queue_wait_seconds=round(flush_seconds_p95*0.20,6)
      | eval lane="hec_splunkd_ui_access"
      | fields _time host pipeline output endpoint tag name chunk_id worker_id flush_seconds_p95 rtt_seconds serialize_seconds queue_wait_seconds lane ]
| eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,kubernetes_cluster,k8s_cluster_name,"undetermined"))))
| eval merge_key=cluster."|".host."|".output."|".lane
| eventstats perc95(flush_seconds_p95) AS cluster_out_p95 median(flush_seconds_p95) AS cluster_out_p50 BY cluster output
| sort 0 merge_key + _time
| streamstats window=7 current=t global=f last(flush_seconds_p95) AS prev_flush last(_time) AS prev_tick BY merge_key
| eval flush_roc_per_min=if(isnotnull(prev_tick) AND prev_tick<_time AND isnotnull(prev_flush), round((flush_seconds_p95-prev_flush)/((_time-prev_tick)/60),6), 0)
| stats max(_time) AS last_seen max(flush_seconds_p95) AS flush_seconds_p95 max(flush_roc_per_min) AS flush_roc_per_min max(rtt_seconds) AS rtt_seconds max(serialize_seconds) AS serialize_seconds max(queue_wait_seconds) AS queue_wait_seconds first(tag) AS tag first(name) AS name first(chunk_id) AS chunk_id first(worker_id) AS worker_id first(pipeline) AS pipeline first(endpoint) AS endpoint first(lane) AS lane max(cluster_out_p95) AS peer_flush_p95 max(cluster_out_p50) AS peer_flush_p50 BY cluster host output
| join type=left max=0 output
    [| inputlookup fluentbit_output_flush_slo_targets.csv
     | eval output=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(output,plugin_key,dest_key,""))))
     | fields output flush_p95_warn_s flush_p95_page_s dest_family ]
| join type=left max=0 host
    [| inputlookup fluentd_node_inventory.csv
     | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(node,host,hostname,""))))
     | fields host cluster_tier owner_team environment ]
| join type=left max=0 host
    [| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu_load FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
     | rename Performance.host AS host ]
| eval flush_p95_warn_s=coalesce(flush_p95_warn_s,2.0)
| eval flush_p95_page_s=coalesce(flush_p95_page_s,7.5)
| eval tail_gap_ratio=if(peer_flush_p50>0, round(flush_seconds_p95/peer_flush_p50,3), null())
| eval severity=case(flush_seconds_p95>=flush_p95_page_s OR flush_roc_per_min>=0.35 OR (tail_gap_ratio>=3.5 AND flush_seconds_p95>=4.0),"page", flush_seconds_p95>=flush_p95_warn_s OR flush_roc_per_min>=0.12 OR (tail_gap_ratio>=2.2 AND flush_seconds_p95>=1.8),"warn", true(),"info")
| eval recommended_action=case(severity=="page" AND match(lane,"hec_"),"inspect_splunk_hec_lb_ack_indexer_queues_increase_hec_throughput_validate_token_pool", severity=="page" AND match(output,"kafka"),"tune_kafka_linger_batch_bytes_broker_latency_partition_skew_and_compression", severity=="page" AND (match(output,"s3") OR match(dest_family,"s3")),"validate_s3_region_endpoint_sts_timeouts_multipart_limits_and_disk_spill", severity=="page" AND (match(output,"elastic") OR match(output,"es")),"check_es_bulk_threadpool_rejections_disk_watermarks_and_index_routing", severity=="page" AND (match(output,"otlp") OR match(output,"otel")),"trace_otlp_grpc_backpressure_codec_batch_and_downstream_collector_saturation", severity=="page" AND match(output,"cloudwatch"),"validate_cloudwatch_putlog_events_batching_throttling_and_iam_role_session", severity=="page","deepen_fluent_bit_output_profiler_net_vs_serialization_vs_queue_wait_raise_workers_under_cpu_budget", severity=="warn","compare_p50_to_p95_tail_expand_http_timing_logs_tune_chunk_size_vs_flush_interval", true(),"refresh_baselines_slo_lookup_and_per_output_latency_dashboards")
| table last_seen cluster host pipeline output endpoint flush_seconds_p95 peer_flush_p50 peer_flush_p95 rtt_seconds serialize_seconds queue_wait_seconds flush_roc_per_min tail_gap_ratio severity recommended_action chunk_id worker_id tag lane cluster_tier owner_team dest_family cpu_load
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu_load latest(Performance.mem_used) AS mem_used FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
| rename Performance.host AS shipper_host
| join type=left max=0 shipper_host
    [| inputlookup fluentd_node_inventory.csv
     | eval shipper_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,node,""))))
     | fields shipper_host cluster_tier owner_team environment ]
| where cpu_load>0 OR mem_used>0
| table shipper_host cpu_load mem_used cluster_tier owner_team environment
```

## Visualization

Heatmap of flush_seconds_p95 by cluster and output, ROC sparklines for flush_roc_per_min, stacked decomposition bars for rtt_seconds serialize_seconds queue_wait_seconds, severity-colored tables with drilldowns to fluentbit:metrics, fluentbit:log, fluentbit:http_timing, and splunkd_ui_access rows.

## Known False Positives

Approved maintenance on Splunk indexers, Elasticsearch data nodes, Kafka brokers, Amazon S3 endpoints, OpenTelemetry Collectors, or CloudWatch control planes routinely lengthens flush tails for minutes without customer-facing loss when retries remain healthy and change windows are documented; require calendar correlation before paging application teams. Rolling Fluent Bit DaemonSet upgrades create transient percentile jitter while old and new serialization paths coexist; suppress brief spikes unless ROC velocity sustains across three windows. Canary clusters replaying historical traffic for forensic drills can inflate p95 independently of production readiness signals. Mis-synchronized clocks between Fluent Bit hosts and Splunk indexers distort rate-of-change math and can mimic false acceleration; monitor NTP stratum health. Very sparse outputs produce unstable percentiles in five-minute buckets; widen spans or require minimum sample counts. Duplicate scrapers emitting two copies of the same metric can double-count in naive averages yet still be harmless if dedupe logic is applied downstream. Cloud provider backbone brownouts may lift network round trip time uniformly across destinations, triggering multiple warn rows that still stem from one external cause. HEC splunkd_ui_access timing records gateway-visible latency rather than always mirroring end-to-end indexer commit latency; pair with platform health dashboards before blaming Fluent Bit alone. Intentional batch size experiments raising serialize_seconds can look like incidents until experiment labels are visible in lookups. Inventory files missing new output aliases route severities to unowned teams until CSV hygiene catches up.
